44.reason: [Using '==' to compare strings checks reference equality, not content. Should use .equals() for string comparison. This causes keywords to be misclassified as identifiers.]

125.reason: [When encountering a single '&', it should report an error but not return a valid token; returning TokenType.AND for "&" is incorrect because "&" alone is not a valid token and should not be added to the token list.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 0.5 , reason:  [When encountering a single '&', it should report an error but not return a valid token; returning TokenType.AND for "&" is incorrect because "&" alone is not a valid token and should not be added to the token list.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 0.5 , reason:  [When encountering a single '&', it should report an error but not return a valid token; returning TokenType.AND for "&" is incorrect because "&" alone is not a valid token and should not be added to the token list.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 0.0625 , reason:  [When encountering a single '&', it should not generate a token; instead, it should only report an error and skip the character. Returning a token causes incorrect output in lexer.txt even for erroneous input.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 0.5 , reason:  [Incorrectly returns a token after reporting an error; lexical analyzer should not produce a valid token for erroneous input like single '&']

134.reason: [Similarly, a single '|' is invalid and should not produce a valid OR token; returning TokenType.OR for "|" incorrectly treats it as a valid lexical unit.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 0.5 , reason:  [Similarly, a single '|' is invalid and should not produce a valid OR token; returning TokenType.OR for "|" incorrectly treats it as a valid lexical unit.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 0.5 , reason:  [Similarly, a single '|' is invalid and should not produce a valid OR token; returning TokenType.OR for "|" incorrectly treats it as a valid token instead of halting or skipping it after error reporting.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 0.0625 , reason:  [Same issue as above: returning a token for a single '|' is incorrect. The lexical analyzer should only report the error and not produce a valid token for invalid symbols.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 0.5 , reason:  [Incorrectly returns a token after reporting an error; lexical analyzer should not produce a valid token for erroneous input like single '|']

199.reason: [Using '==' to compare strings checks reference equality, not content. This will fail to match keywords because string literals may not be interned consistently in all contexts. Should use .equals().]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 0.0625 , reason:  [Using '==' to compare strings checks reference equality, not content. This will fail to match keywords because string literals may not be interned consistently in all contexts. Should use .equals().]
